stem: Triangle ABC has area at least 36 but no more than 54 square units, and side AB has length 6 units. If h represents the length of the altitude from C to side AB and h is an integer, what is one possible value of h?
rationale: The area equals (1/2)·6·h = 3h. The constraint 36 ≤ 3h ≤ 54 gives 12 ≤ h ≤ 18. Since h is an integer, any of 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, or 18 is acceptable.
